value.title: Societal impacts of artificial intelligence: Ethical, legal, and ...
value.content: [My account](/user/login?targetURL=%2Fscience%2Farticle%2Fpii%2FS2949697724000055&from=globalheader). [Sign in](/user/institution/login?targetURL=%2Fscience%2Farticle%2Fpii%2FS2949697724000055). ## [Societal Impacts](/journal/societal-impacts "Go to Societal Impacts on ScienceDirect"). [Volume 3](/journal/societal-impacts/vol/3/suppl/C "Go to table of contents for this volume/issue"), June 2024, 100040. # Societal impacts of artificial intelligence: Ethical, legal, and governance issues. [https://doi.org/10.1016/j.socimp.2024.100040](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.socimp.2024.100040 "Persistent link using digital object identifier")[Get rights and content](https://s100.copyright.com/AppDispatchServlet?publisherName=ELS&contentID=S2949697724000055&orderBeanReset=true). Artificial intelligence (AI) is quickly changing the way we work and the way we live. The societal impact of AI is on most people's minds. This article presents several research projects on how AI impacts work and society. The second study concentrates on bias and discrimination issues embedded in AI applications. It focuses on enhancing the collaboration between AI users and AI systems to alleviate bias and discrimination issues. The third study focuses on the governance of AI, and the study will design and develop an integrated AI governance framework to help guide the design and development of AI applications and facilitate the evolutions and revolutions of ethical AI systems.

value.title: Benefit Versus Harm: The Social and Environmental Impact of AI
value.content: Michael Barrett (University of Cambridge) argues that AI's societal impact hinges on how we govern it. This article examines AI's dual potential
